req.title: Delete patient (soft) (boundary)
req.statement: A system-admin shall soft-delete a patient resource; deletion shall not remove history. Boundary cases on timestamps and numeric elements must be tested.

doc.reasoning: The requirement mandates that a system-admin can soft-delete a Patient resource via DELETE /Patient/{id}, the resource must be marked deleted (not physically removed), history must remain accessible via _history, and non-admin callers must receive 403. Four tests are designed: (1) EP/STC positive: admin soft-deletes an active patient and verifies the deleted marker plus history entry; (2) NEG: non-admin caller receives 403; (3) BVA: delete a patient whose birthDate is at the minimum FHIR date boundary (1900-01-01) and whose id is at the maximum allowed length to stress numeric/timestamp boundary conditions; (4) DT: attempt to delete an already-deleted patient to verify idempotent behaviour and persistent history.

tests.name: test_admin_soft_delete_active_patient
preconditions: Patient resource exists and is active
actions: Create a Patient resource via POST /Patient with admin credentials
Send GET /Patient/{id} and expect 410 Gone or deleted flag
Send GET /Patient/{id}/_history and expect at least two entries
expected: Subsequent GET returns 410 or resource with meta.tag deleted
_history contains the original version and the deleted version
trace: R-HLT-029: deletion shall not remove history
tests.executable:
def test_admin_soft_delete_active_patient(admin_session, base_url, create_patient):
    patient_id = create_patient(admin_session, base_url)

    del_resp = admin_session.delete(f"{base_url}/Patient/{patient_id}")
    assert del_resp.status_code in (200, 204)

    get_resp = admin_session.get(f"{base_url}/Patient/{patient_id}")
    assert get_resp.status_code in (200, 410)
    if get_resp.status_code == 200:
        body = get_resp.json()
        tags = [t.get("code") for t in body.get("meta", {}).get("tag", [])]
        assert "deleted" in tags

    hist_resp = admin_session.get(f"{base_url}/Patient/{patient_id}/_history")
    assert hist_resp.status_code == 200
    entries = hist_resp.json().get("entry", [])
    assert len(entries) >= 2

tests.name: test_non_admin_delete_returns_403
preconditions: Patient resource exists and is active
actions: Create a Patient resource via admin session
Send DELETE /Patient/{id} using a non-admin session
expected: Error body contains OperationOutcome or equivalent
tests.executable:
def test_non_admin_delete_returns_403(admin_session, non_admin_session, base_url, create_patient):
    patient_id = create_patient(admin_session, base_url)

    del_resp = non_admin_session.delete(f"{base_url}/Patient/{patient_id}")
    assert del_resp.status_code == 403

    get_resp = admin_session.get(f"{base_url}/Patient/{patient_id}")
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200
    body = get_resp.json()
    tags = [t.get("code") for t in body.get("meta", {}).get("tag", [])]
    assert "deleted" not in tags

tests.name: test_soft_delete_patient_boundary_birthdate_and_max_id_length
preconditions: FHIR server accepts date 1900-01-01 as a valid birthDate
Patient id length is at the maximum allowed boundary (64 chars per FHIR R4)
actions: Create Patient with birthDate=1900-01-01 and a 64-character id
expected: _history entry for delete version has lastUpdated >= pre-delete timestamp
birthDate value 1900-01-01 preserved in history entry
trace: R-HLT-029: boundary cases on timestamps and numeric elements must be tested
tests.executable:
import datetime

def test_soft_delete_patient_boundary_birthdate_and_max_id_length(
    admin_session, base_url, create_patient_with_payload
):
    boundary_id = "a" * 64
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Patient",
        "id": boundary_id,
        "birthDate": "1900-01-01"
    }
    create_patient_with_payload(admin_session, base_url, payload)

    pre_delete_ts = datetime.datetime.utcnow().replace(microsecond=0)

    del_resp = admin_session.delete(f"{base_url}/Patient/{boundary_id}")
    assert del_resp.status_code in (200, 204)

    hist_resp = admin_session.get(f"{base_url}/Patient/{boundary_id}/_history")
    assert hist_resp.status_code == 200
    entries = hist_resp.json().get("entry", [])
    assert len(entries) >= 2

    deleted_entry = max(
        entries,
        key=lambda e: e.get("resource", {}).get("meta", {}).get("lastUpdated", "")
    )
    last_updated_str = deleted_entry["resource"]["meta"]["lastUpdated"].rstrip("Z")
    last_updated = datetime.datetime.fromisoformat(last_updated_str)
    assert last_updated >= pre_delete_ts

    original_entry = min(
        entries,
        key=lambda e: e.get("resource", {}).get("meta", {}).get("lastUpdated", "")
    )
    assert original_entry["resource"].get("birthDate") == "1900-01-01"

tests.name: test_soft_delete_already_deleted_patient_is_idempotent
preconditions: Patient resource has already been soft-deleted
expected: Second DELETE returns 200, 204, or 404/410 (server may differ)
No server error (5xx) on second delete
trace: R-HLT-029: deletion shall not remove history
tests.executable:
def test_soft_delete_already_deleted_patient_is_idempotent(
    admin_session, base_url, create_patient
):
    patient_id = create_patient(admin_session, base_url)

    first_del = admin_session.delete(f"{base_url}/Patient/{patient_id}")
    assert first_del.status_code in (200, 204)

    second_del = admin_session.delete(f"{base_url}/Patient/{patient_id}")
    assert second_del.status_code < 500, "Server must not return 5xx on repeat delete"
    assert second_del.status_code in (200, 204, 404, 410)

    hist_resp = admin_session.get(f"{base_url}/Patient/{patient_id}/_history")
    assert hist_resp.status_code == 200
    entries = hist_resp.json().get("entry", [])
    assert len(entries) >= 2
    methods = [
        e.get("request", {}).get("method", "")
        for e in entries
    ]
    assert "DELETE" in methods
